research_question: What taxonomy of reasons do poets give, in the moment, for rejecting suggested line endings, and what does the distribution of those reasons reveal about the implicit criteria governing line-ending decisions?
intervention_primitive: Elicitation instrument: a drafting environment that surfaces line-ending alternatives on demand and requires the poet to annotate each rejection with a short spoken or typed reason, treating the tool as a structured interview apparatus rather than a productivity aid.
one_sentence_claim: Rejection reasons collected at the moment of line-ending choice yield a stable, saturated taxonomy of poetic criteria that is not recoverable from post-hoc interviews or from accept-only telemetry.
why_divergent: Differs from branch A on method_family (formative taxonomy-building vs generative mechanism study) and on research_question (implicit criteria vs behavioral divergence); differs from branch B on human_system_relationship (infrastructural_support for elicitation vs peer_collaboration) and on intervention_primitive (annotation-forcing probe vs meter-aware co-writer).

grounding.quoted_claim: Probes are framed as instruments for inspiration and dialogue rather than for extracting generalizable user data.

Cultural probes embrace uncertainty and partial returns rather than seeking comprehensive coverage of user behavior, positioning the artefact as a means of eliciting inspirational fragments from participants.

Domain-specific autonomy comprises skilled competence and authentic value-formation within a domain of expertise, both of which can be eroded by AI decision-support systems that obscure failure indicators or produce unconscious value shifts.

Tools past a certain threshold of scale and complexity reverse from serving human purposes to conditioning humans to serve the tool, contracting rather than expanding the range of autonomous action available to users.
